figures.caption: Table 1: Distinct matchup combinations used in the benchmark schedule. All listed LLMs appear in both Simple and Briefed configurations. The benchmark contains 12 distinct LLM matchup combinations, plus a separate Null ablation matchup against VPAI. Game counts are aggregated across runs with the same normalized matchup composition.
Table 2: Predictive validity metrics on the 5-fold CV vs. the LLM/non-LLM split.
Figure 8: Strategic commitment relative to Vanilla (positive = more concentrated).
Figure 2: Overall (first column) and per-strategy (rest columns) ELO ratings.

Figure 6: Group permutation importance for AttentionMLP by feature group and eventual victory type. Each cell shows the increase in log loss after permuting one semantic feature group; higher values indicate that AttentionMLP relies more heavily on that group for that victory type.
Figure 3: Most chosen strategy, most pivoted-to strategy, and best-ELO strategy.
Figure 15: Distribution of focus briefer count per game for briefed configurations.
Table 4: LLM inference costs by strategist-model pairing.
Figure 11: Pivot-flow heatmaps by player type. Each facet shows from→to transition rates per game, with average win probability at pivot annotated in-cell.
Figure 4: Brier score and log loss by turn-progress decile for all eight models. All models improve as games resolve, with the learned models maintaining the lowest loss throughout.
Figure 5: Within-game rank agreement by turn progress. Agreement is high overall and grows stronger in later phases of the game.
Figure 1: Late-game log loss by victory type and model.
Figure 9: Strategy time allocation (mean share of game time) by player type.
Figure 10: Win probability at strategy pivot.
